title: The Hundred Thousand Songs: Song of Realization
description: Songs of awakening by Tibet's most beloved yogi — who went from murderer to enlightened master in a single lifetime.
introduction: Milarepa (1052-1135) is Tibet's most famous saint — a murderer who through extreme asceticism and devotion to his teacher Marpa achieved complete enlightenment in a single lifetime. His songs, spontaneously composed in mountain caves, express the direct experience of emptiness and luminosity with an immediacy that has made them beloved across all Tibetan Buddhist traditions.

translation.text: I am the cotton-clad Milarepa. In this very body I have realized the nature of reality.
I meditated on the meaning of emptiness, the perfection of wisdom beyond expression.
